title: eriogonum vimineum subsp. polycladon
intro: Eriogonum vimineum subsp. polycladon, also known as Mormon tea or desert briar, is a subspecies of the buckwheat sage plant, characterized by its shrub-like habit and clusters of small, pink or white flowers. Traditionally, Mormon tea has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and acting as a diuretic, making it a potential treatment for conditions like bronchitis and kidney stones. Mormon tea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, often made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, and can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
